title: Estate
description: The root estate record — will, intestacy, or trust-based succession plan. Contains testator details, jurisdiction, will formalities, forced heirship, probate grants, death record, multi-jurisdiction administration, and tax treaty positions.

Codicil.description: An amendment to an existing will. Codicils must be executed with the same formalities as the will itself.

description.description: Summary of what this codicil changes in the original will.
examples: Replaces executor appointment — substitutes daughter for brother-in-law

documentId.description: Reference to the Document entity storing the codicil text.

TestamentaryCapacity.description: Record of testamentary capacity assessment. The testator must understand the nature of making a will, the extent of their estate, and the claims of those they are excluding.

assessed.description: Whether a capacity assessment was carried out.

assessmentDate.description: Date the capacity assessment took place.

assessorName.description: Name of the person who assessed capacity.

assessorRole.description: Professional role of the assessor — typically a GP, psychiatrist, or solicitor.

capacityConfirmed.description: Whether the assessor confirmed the testator has testamentary capacity.

goldenRuleComplied.$comment: The 'golden rule' (Kenward v Adams [1975]) — elderly/seriously ill testators should have a medical practitioner assess capacity.
goldenRuleComplied.description: Whether the 'golden rule' was followed — a medical practitioner assessing capacity for elderly or seriously ill testators.

notes.description: Additional notes on the capacity assessment.
examples: Testator showed clear understanding of estate and family circumstances

ForcedHeirship.description: Forced heirship regime details. Many civil law jurisdictions reserve a portion of the estate for certain heirs regardless of the will.

applies.description: Whether forced heirship rules apply to this estate.

reservedPortion.description: Percentage of the estate reserved for protected heirs. In France, this is 50% for one child, 66.67% for two, 75% for three or more.

disposableQuota.description: Percentage of the estate the testator may freely dispose of by will.

jurisdiction.description: The jurisdiction whose forced heirship rules apply.

claimNature.$comment: property_share: heir receives a share of the actual estate property (France, Germany). cash_claim: heir has a monetary claim against the estate but not a property right (Switzerland post-2023, Japan). usufruct: heir receives a right of use/enjoyment, not ownership (some Spanish regions). court_discretion: court decides the form of provision (England and Wales — Inheritance Act 1975).
claimNature.description: The legal nature of the forced heir's claim — whether they receive property, cash, or a court-determined provision.

calculationBasis.$comment: fixed: a set percentage regardless of family size (Japan: 50% of intestate share). per_child_sliding: varies by number of children (France). court_discretion: no fixed formula — court determines reasonable provision (England and Wales). conditional: depends on specific conditions being met.
calculationBasis.description: How the reserved portion is calculated.

applicableTo.$comment: children_only: only direct children are protected heirs. children_and_spouse: surviving spouse and children are protected. all_descendants: includes grandchildren and further issue. all_heirs: all persons in the intestacy hierarchy (rare).
applicableTo.description: Which categories of heir are protected by forced heirship.

deferralRule.description: Rule for deferring forced heirship claims — e.g. life interest to surviving spouse with remainder to children.
examples: Surviving spouse receives usufruct; children receive bare ownership

effectiveDate.$comment: When these forced heirship rules took effect. Swiss 2023 reform: 2023-01-01.
effectiveDate.description: Date when the applicable forced heirship rules came into force. Important for reform transitions.

reformVersion.description: The specific legislative reform establishing these rules.

notes.description: Additional notes on forced heirship — exceptions, pending challenges, or reform context.
examples: Swiss 2023 reform reduced children's reserved portion from 75% to 50%

AdjudicatingBody.description: A court or authority with jurisdiction over this estate or part of it. Plural legal systems may have multiple adjudicating bodies for the same estate.

type.$comment: secular_court: state civil or probate court. religious_court: a religious tribunal (unspecified denomination). beth_din: Jewish rabbinical court. shariah_court: Islamic law court. tribal_court: indigenous/tribal judicial body. family_court: state family division. family_council: collective family decision-making body (West African, Pacific Island). community_elders: informal elder-led adjudication. partition_meeting: Hindu joint family partition proceedings. karta_decision: decision by the karta (manager) of a Hindu Undivided Family. maori_land_court: New Zealand court for Maori land succession. high_court: national or state high court (typically for contested probate).

name.description: Name of the specific court or body.
examples: Principal Registry of the Family Division

jurisdiction.description: Jurisdiction this adjudicating body operates in.

authoritative.description: Whether this body's decisions are binding. A beth din ruling may be persuasive but not enforceable without secular court recognition.

ParallelDistribution.description: A parallel distribution scheme operating alongside the primary estate distribution. Common in plural legal systems where statutory and customary/religious distributions run concurrently.

system.description: The legal system governing this parallel distribution.

jurisdiction.description: Jurisdiction where this parallel distribution applies.

description.description: Summary of how this system distributes the estate.
examples: Family compound passes to eldest son as custodian under Igbo customary law
Customary distribution of family land conflicts with testamentary gift to daughter

ProbateGrant.description: A grant of probate or letters of administration issued by a court. Without a grant, executors have no legal authority to administer the estate.

examples: Letters of Administration with Will Annexed

grantDate.description: Date the grant was issued by the court.

grantReference.description: Court reference number for the grant.

issuingCourt.description: Name of the court or registry that issued the grant.
examples: Principal Registry of the Family Division
Supreme Court of New South Wales

DeathRecord.description: Details of the testator's death. Establishes the date from which succession takes effect and the domicile at death (which determines governing law for moveables).

dateOfDeath.description: Date of death in ISO 8601 format.

domicileAtDeath.description: The testator's domicile at the time of death. This determines which succession law governs moveable property.

notes.description: Additional notes about the death record.
examples: Death registered in two jurisdictions — UK and France

SuccessionConflict.description: A conflict between legal systems regarding succession for this estate. Common in plural jurisdictions and cross-border estates.

description.description: Summary of the conflict between legal systems.
examples: Statutory will gives daughter the house; Igbo customary law gives eldest son custodianship
French forced heirship claim conflicts with English testamentary freedom

resolutionStatus.$comment: unresolved: no resolution attempt yet. resolved: conflict has been settled. pending_court: matter referred to a court. pending_arbitration: matter referred to arbitration or mediation.

examples: Family council meeting scheduled for December 2025

id.description: Unique identifier for this estate record.

testatorPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the testator (the person whose estate this is).

status.$comment: planning: estate plan is being prepared, not yet finalised. confirmed: estate plan is signed and in effect — the will has been executed. pre_probate: testator has died, estate is being gathered before applying for probate/grant. in_administration: grant obtained, estate is being administered — collecting assets, paying debts, distributing. distributed: all assets distributed to beneficiaries, final accounts pending. closed: estate administration is complete — all accounts filed, all distributions confirmed.
status.description: The lifecycle status of this estate record. Progresses from planning through to closure.

jurisdiction.description: Primary jurisdiction governing this estate. For cross-border estates, this is the domiciliary jurisdiction.

willType.$comment: secular: standard statutory will. religious: will made under a religious legal system (Shariah, halacha). dual: separate secular and religious wills for the same estate. composite: a single will incorporating both secular and religious elements. oral_witnessed: spoken will witnessed by required number of witnesses. oral_customary: spoken will valid under customary law. holographic: handwritten will entirely in the testator's hand (valid in France, Japan, some US states). notarised: will executed before a notary public (standard in civil law jurisdictions). privileged_will: military or mariner's will with relaxed formalities.
willType.description: The type of will governing this estate. Determines which formality rules apply.

extensionWillType.$comment: Non-core will types (e.g. jihi_shosho for Japanese holographic wills). Extension schemas validate.
extensionWillType.description: Extension-specific will type not covered by the core enum. Validated by the relevant extension schema.

companionEstateId.$comment: For dual-system estates — links secular and religious estate records. Also used for companion (household/couples) estates.
companionEstateId.description: Links to a companion estate record — either a parallel estate under a different legal system, or a household companion's estate.

companionLinkStatus.description: Current state of the companion estate link. Absent if the estate has never been linked to a companion.
companionLinkStatus.$comment: Lifecycle: invited → active → decoupling → decoupled. See docs/companion-estates.md for full semantics.

linkedAt.description: ISO 8601 date when the companion link became active.

decoupledAt.description: ISO 8601 date when the companion link was severed.

primaryInstrument.$comment: will: estate passes primarily through the will. revocable_trust: estate passes through a revocable (living) trust — common in US estate planning. both: combination of will and trust. intestacy: no will exists — estate distributes under intestacy rules.
primaryInstrument.description: The primary instrument governing succession for this estate.

defaultPropertyRegime.$comment: community_property: assets acquired during marriage are jointly owned 50/50 (US community property states, France, Spain). separate_property: each spouse owns what they acquire (England and Wales, most common law). equitable_distribution: court divides property fairly on divorce/death (most US states on divorce). deferred_community: assets are separate during marriage but shared on dissolution (Scandinavia, Germany). universal_community: all assets are joint from marriage (Netherlands pre-2018, some Indonesian adat). participation_in_acquisitions: Swiss regime — separate during marriage, share gains on dissolution. islamic_dower: mahr-based system — property is separate, wife has deferred mahr claim.
defaultPropertyRegime.description: The default marital property regime for this estate. Determines which assets the testator may freely dispose of.

totalEstimatedValue.description: Estimated total value of the estate before liabilities. In minor currency units.

attestation.description: Will execution formalities — witnesses, signing method, and compliance checks.

testamentaryCapacity.description: Record of testamentary capacity assessment for the testator.

codicils.description: Amendments to the will. Each codicil must be executed with proper formalities.

revokesDocumentIds.description: Document IDs of prior wills or instruments this estate record expressly revokes.

revocationClause.description: Whether the will contains a general revocation clause ('I revoke all former wills and testamentary dispositions').

forcedHeirship.description: Forced heirship regime applicable to this estate, if any.

adjudicatingBodies.description: Courts and bodies with jurisdiction over this estate. Plural legal systems may involve multiple courts.

parallelDistributions.description: Parallel distribution schemes under different legal systems — customary, religious, or statutory.

probateGrant.description: The probate grant or letters of administration for this estate.

successionConflicts.description: Known conflicts between legal systems regarding succession for this estate.

choiceOfLaw.$comment: Brussels IV Regulation choice of law. Allows EU nationals to choose their nationality's law for succession.
choiceOfLaw.description: Choice of law declaration under the Brussels IV Regulation (EU Succession Regulation 650/2012) or equivalent. Allows the testator to choose which jurisdiction's succession law applies.

chosenLaw.description: The jurisdiction whose law the testator has chosen to govern succession.

basis.description: Legal basis for the choice of law.
examples: Brussels IV Regulation Art. 22 — nationality at time of choice
Hague Convention on the Law Applicable to Succession

documentRef.description: Reference to the document containing the choice of law declaration.

electiveShareRight.$comment: US — surviving spouse's right to elect against the will.
electiveShareRight.description: Surviving spouse's elective share right (US). Allows the spouse to claim a statutory share of the estate regardless of the will's provisions.

available.description: Whether elective share is available in this jurisdiction.

electedAmount.description: The amount elected by the surviving spouse, if the election has been made.

statute.description: The statute governing elective share in this jurisdiction.

notes.description: Additional notes on the elective share.
examples: Augmented estate calculation includes revocable trust assets

priorMatrimonialClaim.description: A prior matrimonial claim against the estate — typically from a former spouse under a financial order.

exists.description: Whether a prior matrimonial claim exists.

examples: Former wife has lump sum order of £50,000 outstanding from 2018 divorce

claimantPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the claimant.

examples: Clean break order obtained — no further claims

noContestClause.$comment: In terrorem clause — beneficiary forfeits gift if they challenge the will.
noContestClause.description: In terrorem (no-contest) clause — a provision that a beneficiary forfeits their gift if they challenge the will. Enforceability varies by jurisdiction.

present.description: Whether the will contains a no-contest clause.

enforceability.description: Enforceability status in the governing jurisdiction.
examples: Generally unenforceable in England and Wales
Clause includes safe harbour for challenges brought in good faith

pretermittedHeirClaims.$comment: Claims by children born/adopted after the will was made.
pretermittedHeirClaims.description: Claims by pretermitted heirs — children born or adopted after the will was executed who are not mentioned in it.

claimantPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the pretermitted heir.

examples: Child born 2023, will executed 2020 — no mention in will or codicil

testamentaryPromises.$comment: NZ — claims under the Law Reform (Testamentary Promises) Act 1949.
testamentaryPromises.description: Claims under the Law Reform (Testamentary Promises) Act 1949 (New Zealand). Allows claims by persons who provided services to the deceased in reliance on a promise of testamentary provision.

claimantPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the claimant.

promiseDescription.description: Description of the promise made by the testator.
examples: Promised to leave the farm to nephew in return for 10 years of farm management
Multiple witnesses to the promise — strong claim

judicialVariationPower.$comment: UK/NZ — court power to vary a will under the Inheritance (Provision for Family and Dependants) Act 1975 or equivalent.
judicialVariationPower.description: Court power to vary a will to make reasonable financial provision for family and dependants. Applies in England and Wales (Inheritance Act 1975), New Zealand, and similar jurisdictions.

available.description: Whether judicial variation is available in this jurisdiction.

examples: Inheritance (Provision for Family and Dependants) Act 1975
Claim must be brought within 6 months of grant of representation

commorientesRule.$comment: Rule determining order of death when people die in the same event. Varies by jurisdiction.
commorientesRule.description: Commorientes rule — the legal presumption for order of death when two or more people die in the same event. Critical for determining which estate inherits from which.

applicable.description: Whether the commorientes rule is relevant to this estate.

examples: s.184 Law of Property Act 1925 — deaths presumed in order of seniority
Uniform Simultaneous Death Act — treated as if each predeceased the other

notes.description: Additional context — e.g. specific facts of the case.
examples: Husband (72) and wife (68) died in same road traffic accident

ancillaryProbate.$comment: Multi-jurisdiction estate administration. Each entry represents one jurisdiction's administration.
ancillaryProbate.description: Ancillary probate proceedings in jurisdictions other than the primary domiciliary jurisdiction. Required when the estate includes assets in foreign jurisdictions.

jurisdiction.description: The jurisdiction where ancillary probate is needed.

fiduciaryPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the local fiduciary or administrator.

status.$comment: not_started: ancillary probate has not yet been initiated. applied: application has been submitted. granted: grant has been issued. completed: administration in this jurisdiction is finished. waived: ancillary probate is not required (e.g. small estate exemption).
status.description: Current status of ancillary probate in this jurisdiction.

localCounsel.description: Name of local legal counsel handling this jurisdiction.

examples: French notaire requires apostilled UK grant

registrations.$comment: Will registries worldwide (DIFC Wills Service Centre, UK Probate Registry, etc.).
registrations.description: Registrations of the will with official will registries. Not all jurisdictions have will registries, and registration is rarely compulsory.

jurisdictionScope.description: What the registration covers — may be limited to certain jurisdictions or asset classes.

reportingCurrency.description: The currency used for probate reporting and estate accounts. All values in this estate may need converting to this currency.

conversionRates.$comment: Exchange rates used for estate valuation in cross-border estates.
conversionRates.description: Exchange rates used when converting foreign-currency assets to the reporting currency. Document the rate and date to satisfy probate courts.

rate.description: Exchange rate — one unit of fromCurrency equals this many units of toCurrency.

rateDate.description: Date this exchange rate was sourced.

taxTreatyPositions.$comment: Bilateral tax treaty claims relevant to the estate.
taxTreatyPositions.description: Tax treaty positions relevant to the estate. Documents which treaties are being relied upon for double taxation relief.

treaty.description: Name of the bilateral tax treaty.

primaryTaxingJurisdiction.description: The jurisdiction with primary taxing rights under this treaty.

claimedCredits.description: Tax credits claimed under this treaty.
examples: Credit for UK IHT against US federal estate tax

relevantArticle.description: The specific treaty article being relied upon.

examples: Treaty predates FATCA — potential interaction with reporting requirements

suggestedPractitionerNeeds.$comment: Informational. Activity codes computed from estate complexity — e.g. trust_preparation, cross_border, iht_planning. Canonical taxonomy at openinherit.org/reference/practitioner-activities.json.
suggestedPractitionerNeeds.description: Informational list of practitioner activity codes suggesting the types of professional help this estate may need. Computed from estate complexity, not user-entered.

createdAt.description: Date this estate record was first created.

lastModifiedAt.description: Date this estate record was last modified.

executionDate.description: Date the will was formally executed (signed with proper attestation). Enables look-back period calculations when combined with lifetime transfers.

administration.$comment: Administration phase data — populated when the testator dies and the estate enters pre_probate or in_administration status.
administration.description: Estate administration details. Tracks the journey from death through grant, tax clearance, distribution, and closure.

dateOfDeath.description: Date of death — the date from which succession takes effect.

dateOfGrant.description: Date the probate grant or letters of administration were issued.

grantType.$comment: grant_of_probate: E&W/AU/NZ — court confirms named executor. letters_of_administration: E&W — no will, court appoints administrator. letters_of_administration_with_will_annexed: E&W — will exists but named executor cannot act. succession_certificate: IN/PK — Indian Succession Act s.372. certificate_of_inheritance: DE/JP/FR — Erbschein (DE), 相続証明書 (JP), Acte de notoriété (FR). court_appointment: generic court appointment of administrator. resealing: foreign grant recognised locally (Commonwealth). european_certificate_of_succession: EU Brussels IV certificate. other: jurisdiction-specific grant type.
grantType.description: Type of grant authorising estate administration.

grantReference.description: Court reference number for the grant.

court.description: Name of the issuing court or registry.
examples: Principal Registry of the Family Division

grossEstateValue.description: Gross value of the estate at date of death, before deducting liabilities.

netEstateValue.description: Net value of the estate after deducting liabilities.

taxPaid.description: Amount of inheritance/estate tax paid so far.

taxClearance.description: Tax clearance status — confirmation from the tax authority that no further tax is owed.

status.$comment: not_applied: clearance not yet applied for. applied: application submitted. received: clearance certificate received. not_required: estate below tax threshold.

distributions.description: Record of distributions made to beneficiaries during administration.

beneficiaryPersonId.description: Person ID of the beneficiary receiving the distribution.

bequestId.description: Reference to the Bequest being fulfilled.

assetId.description: Asset ID if a specific asset was transferred.

propertyId.description: Property ID if a property was transferred.

cashAmount.description: Cash amount distributed, in minor currency units.

receipt.description: Whether a receipt/acknowledgement has been obtained from the beneficiary.

examples: Interim distribution — further distribution expected after property sale

estateAccountsFiled.description: Whether the estate accounts have been prepared and filed/approved.

examples: Awaiting sale of Spanish property before final distribution

notes.description: Free-text notes about the estate. Use for anything not captured by structured fields.
examples: Complex cross-border estate — UK, France, and Singapore assets. Dual will structure.
